projectDescription: A Spring Boot TCP server that receives GT06 protocol GPS device data over TCP/Netty, decodes it, and forwards it to a backend via HTTP.
keyFeatures: GT06 protocol TCP server using Netty for high-performance GPS device communication
IMEI-based device authentication with login ACK/NACK
Real-time GPS location decoding (latitude, longitude, speed, validity)
Heartbeat handling with ACC status tracking
HTTP forwarding of decoded GPS data to a configurable backend service
Dockerized deployment with Spring Boot on Java 17
publicApiSurface: POST /data/ubicacion — Receive a GPSData payload and forward to backend
TCP port 9000 — GT06 protocol: Login (0x01), Location (0x12), Heartbeat (0x13)
GT06Utils.ACK(short serial) — Build GT06 login ACK response
GT06Utils.ACKHeartbeat(short serial) — Build GT06 heartbeat ACK response
SendHTTP.EnviaDatos(GPSData) — POST GPS data to backend URL
